topic: The Monitoring Illusion — Section rewrite review (EXP-080 integration)
change_summary: EXP-080 integrated: corrected live-fetch count from 6 to 4, added direct monitor.py code quotes from commit 8e4bf9b, named live-fetch WINs with data sources, added development trajectory paragraph, added stratification of unmonitored WINs, clarified accuracy figure origin.
our_argument_summary: Section 2b.1 argues the dome's monitoring dashboard is an illusion: the accuracy figure is manually entered HTML, most WINs use hardcoded validation where pred==obs, only 4 of 70 fetch live data (and those rely on globe-calibrated sources), and the development trajectory trends toward more hardcoding. The rewrite adds direct code quotes (Schumann 7.83/7.83, SAA Decay 77.0/77.0, Crepuscular Ray 5733/5733) and a stratification of unmonitored WINs by category. The core argument is sound and significantly strengthened by the rewrite.
improvements_since_cycle_1: Corrected live_fetch count from 6 to 4 — now matches wins.json tags exactly (WIN-004, 006, 007, 024)
Added direct monitor.py code quotes with specific commit hash (8e4bf9b) and full GitHub link — addresses Cycle 1 critical hole about lacking code evidence
Named all 4 live-fetch WINs with their data sources — no longer an anonymous statistic
Added development trajectory paragraph showing recent domains trend toward more hardcoding — strengthens the argument
Added stratification of unmonitored WINs — addresses Cycle 1 moderate hole about treating all unmonitored WINs as equivalent
Clarified accuracy figure is .score-number CSS class, not JavaScript — eliminates the 'computed then published' defense

holes_found.description: TLDR overgeneralizes the 50% error floor. The TLDR says 'even those use adaptive tolerances with 50% error floors' — implying all 4 live-fetch WINs have this tolerance floor. But the body text only documents the 50% floor for the NMP Drift Rate domain (WIN-006/007). The SAA separation (WIN-004) and Roaring 40s (WIN-024) domains may have different tolerance settings. A dome defender could check and find that WIN-004 doesn't have a 50% floor, undermining the TLDR's accuracy. Per project rules, TLDR factual errors are major because readers see TLDRs first and may not expand.
holes_found.recommendation: Change TLDR to be precise: 'Only {{CA_LIVE}} WINs fetch live external data, and even those rely on globe-calibrated data sources; one uses an adaptive tolerance with a 50% error floor.' Or verify whether all 4 live-fetch domains have the 50% floor before claiming it universally.

holes_found.description: Self-contradicted WINs miscounted in the stratification paragraph. The paragraph on 'Stratifying the unmonitored WINs' says 'Nine are self-contradicted by the dome's own geometry (WIN-045/046/049/050/051 tidal, WIN-053 flux, WIN-056 solar, WIN-061 P-wave, WIN-067 gravity).' But WIN-045, WIN-046, and WIN-049 have monitoring=hardcoded in wins.json, NOT monitoring=none. They are not 'unmonitored' — they have hardcoded monitoring domains. The correct count of self-contradicted WINs with monitoring=none is 8: WIN-044, 050, 051, 053, 056, 061, 067, 068. Three hardcoded WINs are incorrectly claimed as unmonitored, while WIN-044 and WIN-068 are missing from the list. A dome defender who looks up WIN-045's monitoring domain will find it exists, undermining our credibility on this stratification.
holes_found.recommendation: Fix the stratification paragraph to correctly list only the 8 self-contradicted WINs that have monitoring=none. Additionally, note that 8 OTHER self-contradicted WINs (WIN-002, 010, 012, 029, 038, 045, 046, 049) DO have monitoring domains — but hardcoded to pass. This is actually a STRONGER argument: the dome doesn't just ignore self-contradicted WINs, it actively validates some of them with checks that cannot fail.

holes_found.description: Misleading WIN count is wrong. The stratification says 'Twelve are misleading (duplicated, circular, or non-discriminating).' But wins.json shows 15 WINs with verdict=Misleading and monitoring=none. The hardcoded number 12 doesn't match the data. This was likely correct at an earlier WIN count but has not been updated.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the hardcoded '12' with a template variable or recount from current data. The correct count is 15 misleading + unmonitored WINs. Given the project rule against hardcoding counts, consider whether this stratification paragraph should use computed values.

holes_found.description: The sentence 'the dome has built monitoring infrastructure for {{CA_HARDCODED}} WINs (all hardcoded to pass) but does not monitor the 9 WINs whose own geometry produces wrong answers' is factually wrong. 8 of 16 self-contradicted WINs ARE in the hardcoded category (WIN-002, 010, 012, 029, 038, 045, 046, 049). The dome has built monitoring for them — it just hardcodes the pass. This undercuts the contrast the sentence tries to draw between 'monitored (hardcoded)' and 'self-contradicted (unmonitored)'. The reality is even more damning: the dome hardcodes passes for WINs whose geometry contradicts itself.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite to: 'The dome has built monitoring infrastructure for {{CA_HARDCODED}} WINs (all hardcoded to pass) — including 8 WINs whose own geometry produces self-contradictory results (Part 2). Meanwhile, another 8 self-contradicted WINs have no monitoring code at all. Whether hardcoded to pass or silently omitted, no self-contradicted WIN faces a genuine empirical test.'

holes_found.description: The accuracy arithmetic '67/(67+4+5) ≈ 88.2%' is hardcoded and potentially stale. The '67' doesn't match the dome's current V51.1 claim of 69 WINs. The '5' doesn't match uncounted-failures.json which now has 8 entries. The dome's own accuracy_variants data shows multiple alternative calculations (96.3%, 100%, 89.3%, 94.7%) depending on data source. The section picks one alternative and presents it without explaining where 67 and 5 come from. A dome defender could challenge: 'Where do you get 67? We claim 69. Where do you get 5? Show us.' The project rule says never hardcode computed counts.
holes_found.recommendation: Either use template variables for the alternative accuracy calculation, or explicitly state the methodology: '67 confirmed WINs (excluding 2 below-detection-threshold) divided by (67 + 4 dome-acknowledged failures + N uncounted failures) = X%.' Better yet, use the dome's own wins-page arithmetic that yields 89.3% — this is the dome's own data contradicting its headline number.

holes_found.description: The stratification paragraph omits several verdict categories among unmonitored WINs. The 41 unmonitored WINs break down as: 8 Self-Contradicted, 15 Misleading, 11 Std Model Explains, 3 Refuted by Data, 2 Not Demonstrated, 2 Unfalsifiable. The section only mentions theological (2), self-contradicted (incorrectly 9), and misleading (incorrectly 12). The 11 'Std Model Explains' and 3 'Refuted by Data' WINs are lumped into 'The remaining WINs are quantitative predictions the dome could monitor but chooses not to.' This understates the variety of problems.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand the stratification to cover the full breakdown, or at minimum mention that 11 unmonitored WINs are standard-physics relabels (monitoring would confirm the phenomenon but not the dome's causal explanation) and 3 are refuted by published data (monitoring would expose the failure). This sharpens the argument: the dome's monitoring gaps are systematically located where monitoring would be most damaging.

holes_found.description: The TLDR says '{{CA_HARDCODED}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} monitored domains' but the template variables refer to WIN counts (25 and 70), not monitoring domain counts (39). There are 39 monitor.py domains but 70 WINs. Saying '25 of 70 monitored domains' is technically inaccurate — 25 WINs have hardcoded domains, but the total domain count is 39, not 70.
holes_found.recommendation: Change TLDR from 'monitored domains' to 'reviewed WINs' or 'WINs': '{{CA_HARDCODED}} of {{CA_REVIEWED}} WINs compare static predicted values against static observed values.'

stronger_arguments.description: The strongest untapped argument from the data: 8 of 16 self-contradicted WINs have hardcoded monitoring domains. The dome doesn't just fail to monitor its contradictions — it actively monitors them and hardcodes the pass. This is the difference between negligence and construction. A monitoring system that ignores problematic WINs is incomplete; one that includes them with unfalsifiable checks is designed to mask problems. Frame the self-contradicted + hardcoded combination as the most revealing data point in the whole section.
sources: wins.json: 8 WINs with verdict=Self-Contradicted AND monitoring=hardcoded (002, 010, 012, 029, 038, 045, 046, 049)

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own internal data sources disagree with each other on accuracy: api/scorecard.json says 96.3%, api/current/results.json says 100%, wins page arithmetic gives 89.3%, and the headline claims 94.5%. Four different numbers from the same model's own infrastructure. This isn't just a hardcoded number — it's an internally inconsistent one. The section could note that even if the accuracy figure WERE computed, the model's own data sources don't agree on what it should be.
sources: uncounted-failures.json: dome_accuracy_variants field showing 4 different accuracy figures from dome's own endpoints

kernel_of_truth.description: The rewrite genuinely strengthens the section. Adding direct code quotes (Schumann 7.83/7.83, SAA Decay 77.0/77.0) with a verifiable commit hash makes the argument concrete and defensible. Naming the 4 live-fetch WINs with data sources (NOAA/INTERMAGNET, NOAA NP.xy, CPC/NOAA AAO) is exactly what Cycle 1 asked for. The development trajectory argument — that recent domains are MORE hardcoded — is a genuinely powerful new point. The stratification of unmonitored WINs is the right structural move even if the current numbers are wrong.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The factual errors in the stratification paragraph (miscounted self-contradicted and misleading WINs, inclusion of hardcoded WINs in the unmonitored list) are the kind of errors a dome defender will find first, because defenders look for specific WIN IDs they can check. Getting WIN-045's monitoring tag wrong when the whole section is about monitoring tags is a credibility-damaging error that could overshadow the otherwise strong analysis.

deeper_analysis:
The EXP-080 rewrite is a substantial improvement over the Cycle 1 text. The core structural critique — hardcoded validation masquerading as live monitoring — is now supported by direct code evidence rather than indirect tag statistics. The commit-pinned code quotes make the argument verifiable and defensible.

However, the stratification paragraph introduced factual errors that are more dangerous than the problems it fixes. The paragraph claims WIN-045/046/049 are 'unmonitored' when they have hardcoded monitoring domains. This is exactly the kind of error a dome defender will check first: 'You say WIN-045 has no monitoring, but I can show you the domain in monitor.py.' We'd lose credibility on the entire section because of a detail error in one paragraph.

The stronger framing is: '8 self-contradicted WINs have hardcoded monitoring (the dome builds checks for them that cannot fail), and 8 more have no monitoring at all. Either way, no self-contradicted WIN faces a genuine test.' This is actually more damning than the current text.

The accuracy arithmetic should ideally use template variables. The dome's own data disagrees with itself (96.3%, 100%, 89.3%, 94.5% from four internal sources), which is a powerful point the section could exploit.

Overall verdict: the section's core argument is strong and well-evidenced. The stratification paragraph needs factual corrections before a dome defender finds the errors. Recommended action: minor_edit (fix the three wrong numbers and the TLDR overgeneralization). The structural argument doesn't need rewriting — just the specific counts and WIN lists.

citations_verified: Commit hash 8e4bf9b exists as a full URL to john09289/predictions repo — format is valid
Schumann Frequency = 7.83 Hz — standard physics value, correct
SAA Decay Rate = 77.0 — matches dome raw text reference
Crepuscular Ray = 5733 — matches dome's sun_altitude parameter
NOAA/INTERMAGNET as data source for SAA monitoring — standard geomagnetic sources
CPC/NOAA AAO index for Roaring 40s — correct identification
.score-number CSS class for accuracy display — plausible HTML structure claim
citations_unchecked: Whether commit 8e4bf9b still exists at that URL (dome author could force-push or delete)
Whether the 39 monitoring domains count is still accurate in V51.1 vs V51.0
Whether the 'static win' comments actually exist in domains 16-39 of current monitor.py
The OPEN-001 through OPEN-015 claim of '15 unresolved open problems' — not verified against current dome site
Whether effective_tol = max(adaptive_tol, 50.0) applies only to NMP Drift Rate or to other live-fetch domains too
